jokes.text: Which included a record-tying... three lovemaking sessions in one night for Michael
jokes.setup: Michael discussing how well things are going with Sally
jokes.punchline: Narrator's matter-of-fact delivery of Michael's sexual performance stats
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Michael's awkward pride mixed with narrator's clinical tone. Sets up major callback.

jokes.text: There isn't? This is unbelievable. Can I get a virgin piña colada when you get a chance?
jokes.setup: George Michael worried about being on his third Virgin Mary
jokes.punchline: He's shocked there's no alcohol and immediately orders another virgin drink
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Michael innocence - he's trying to be rebellious but doesn't understand what he's rebelling against.

jokes.text: - Who? - W-Ann. - Yes. Yes. - Yeah, we had- Of course I know Ann.
jokes.setup: George Michael mentions his girlfriend Ann
jokes.punchline: Michael clearly doesn't remember Ann exists despite claiming to know her
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of a running gag. Michael's awkward recovery is beautifully written and performed.

jokes.text: I like not having fun. I like your idea of fun, I mean. Our idea of fun. I like not having that.
jokes.setup: Ann criticizes George Michael for having fun instead of going to church
jokes.punchline: George Michael's increasingly convoluted attempt to agree with Ann's joyless worldview
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - shows George Michael's desperation to please Ann leading to logical pretzels.

jokes.text: Oh, big deal, three times. I've had three times plenty of times. Yes! Did you call my name? Absolutely not.
jokes.setup: Lindsay downplays Michael's sexual achievement
jokes.punchline: She immediately contradicts herself by yelling in pleasure, then denying it
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and character work - Lindsay's competitiveness immediately undermined by reality.

jokes.text: Look at this delicious piece of irony. I'm being sued for divorce.
jokes.setup: Gob enters eating something and transitions to his problems
jokes.punchline: Uses 'delicious' to describe both food and irony, showing his misunderstanding of irony
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob - misusing sophisticated vocabulary while being completely unsophisticated.

jokes.text: She was a seal dealer. She raised and sold seals to aquariums and marine parks
jokes.setup: Gob trying to remember his wife
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and unusual profession of seal dealing
jokes.explanation: Sets up the seal plotline beautifully. The specificity makes it funnier and more believable in AD's world.

jokes.text: There's a reason you don't typically see live seals in a magic show. - So with a simple wave of my- - [Barking]
jokes.setup: Gob explaining why he released the seals, mentions magic show
jokes.punchline: Seal barking interrupts his magic demonstration, proving his point
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual timing and foreshadowing. The interruption proves Gob's point while setting up the loose seal danger.

jokes.text: Bad example- if her name's Amy, I'll call her Blamy.
jokes.setup: Gob planning to make a pun on his wife's name
jokes.punchline: His example pun makes no sense - 'Blamy' isn't a pun on Amy
jokes.explanation: Pure Gob logic - confidently demonstrates something he doesn't understand. The specificity of 'Blamy' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: First of all, it wasn't Michael Moore. It was a look-alike. And it wasn't in front of the whole country. It was for a bit on Jimmy Kimmel Live.
jokes.setup: Lucille claiming Michael Moore confronted her on national TV
jokes.punchline: Each detail is progressively less impressive - not Michael Moore, not national TV, just a late night bit
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - systematically deflates Lucille's dramatic story with mundane reality.

jokes.text: We haven't done this since the Balboa Bay Window magazine
jokes.setup: Lucille surprised by photographer
jokes.punchline: References their last photo shoot for a presumably local/small publication with a punny name
jokes.explanation: Subtle character detail - shows Lucille's vanity and social climbing through magazine reference.

jokes.text: Whatever happened to 'Keepin' It Fresh'? - I was 30 years old then, Mother. I'm 32 now!
jokes.setup: Lucille references some old motto of Buster's
jokes.punchline: Buster acts like two years is a lifetime of change when he's still completely dependent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment - treating minimal change as profound growth while remaining completely infantile.

jokes.text: In fact, there are thousands of books written on child-rearing. But Lucille was in denial.
jokes.setup: Lucille claims kids don't come with handbooks
jokes.punchline: Narrator dryly corrects her - there are literally thousands of parenting books
jokes.explanation: Classic AD narrator correction - punctures character delusions with factual reality.

jokes.text: Actually, she died. - You're kidding. I've been taking credit for that for years. - Credit?
jokes.setup: Barry claims he got Michael divorced
jokes.punchline: He's been taking credit for someone's death as a professional achievement
jokes.explanation: Dark and perfectly in character for incompetent Barry. The word 'credit' makes it especially twisted.

jokes.text: Save it for the stand, okay, Tom Jane?
jokes.setup: Gob expressing emotion about wanting his wife back
jokes.punchline: Barry dismisses it with a random actor reference that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Barry's incompetence showing through random celebrity name-dropping that doesn't fit the situation.

jokes.text: It was with her he'd first set the three-time record. Yes!
jokes.setup: Narrator explaining Michael's history with Maggie
jokes.punchline: Brings back the sexual achievement record, treating it like a sports statistic
jokes.explanation: Good callback execution - narrator's clinical delivery makes the sexual reference funnier.

jokes.text: It's not my belly we have to worry about, Your Honor. It's Loretta's. Being tempted day in and day out by those Skip's Scrambles.
jokes.setup: Maggie appearing very pregnant in court
jokes.punchline: She's using her pregnancy as a prop for the case about the restaurant's tempting food
jokes.explanation: Clever legal maneuvering that ties the pregnancy subplot to the restaurant case. Good callback integration.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I know I'm not supposed to do this... but we just spend so much time in that box. - I knitted you something.
jokes.setup: Juror apologizing for approaching Maggie
jokes.punchline: The intimate gesture of knitting something personal for the attorney
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - inappropriate jury behavior that Maggie can't refuse without seeming ungrateful.

jokes.text: Eight and a half months. Wow. Really? Could've sworn it was a year.
jokes.setup: Michael learning Maggie is 8.5 months pregnant and they were together 8.5 months ago
jokes.punchline: His desperate attempt to convince himself the timing doesn't match
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Michael's denial and mathematical reasoning failing him simultaneously.

jokes.text: Don't worry. You're not the father, Michael. - [Exhales] - No problem. - Really? - Right. - You sure? I mean, uh- I could be.
jokes.setup: Maggie tells Michael he's not the father
jokes.punchline: Michael's relief immediately turns to disappointment and insistence he could be
jokes.explanation: Brilliant psychology - Michael can't handle either possibility and contradicts his own relief.

jokes.text: You can hurl a Bible at the baby.
jokes.setup: Maggie sarcastically inviting Michael to the baptism
jokes.punchline: Suggests violence against a baby with a religious object at a religious ceremony
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and economical. The specificity of 'hurl a Bible' makes the absurdity funnier.

jokes.text: All I could see was her giant chest- box. She had this- Some people call it a chest. I call it a box.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining he didn't notice Maggie was pregnant
jokes.punchline: His attempt to correct himself from 'chest' to 'box' makes it worse and more confusing
jokes.explanation: Michael's awkwardness in trying to be appropriate makes him sound more inappropriate.

jokes.text: All I could see was her giant chest- box. She had this- Some people call it a chest. I call it a box.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining what he noticed about Maggie
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to cover his sexual observation with technical terminology
jokes.explanation: Michael's uncomfortable verbal gymnastics to avoid saying he was staring at her breasts. The awkward correction makes it more obvious what he was thinking.

jokes.text: You know what I do, Mike? Check their urine. Make 'em pee in a cup, and pee in a cup right in front of me.
jokes.setup: Michael wondering how to verify pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Gob's solution is invasive, weird, and redundant ('pee in a cup' twice)
jokes.explanation: Pure Gob - overly confident about a creepy and impractical solution.

jokes.text: Seems like only yesterday you were bursting forth from your mother's fertile womb.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to cover for talking about creating Maeby
jokes.punchline: His attempt at a normal parental comment is weirdly clinical and sexual
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias - trying to sound normal but making everything more uncomfortable with clinical language.

jokes.text: We will be like Tracy and Hepburn. - Yeah! - 'What do you say, you old poop?' - Oh, God, he's Hepburn.
jokes.setup: Lindsay suggests they'll be like the classic movie couple
jokes.punchline: Tobias immediately takes the feminine role with a Katharine Hepburn impression
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Tobias's gender confusion played through classic movie references.

jokes.text: You're high! - Oh, well, you can win every argument like that, but that does not make you right. - I need macaroons. - Oh, you're high!
jokes.setup: Oscar accusing Lucille of being high
jokes.punchline: She makes a reasonable point then immediately proves him right by demanding macaroons
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Lucille's reasonable response immediately undermined by drug-seeking behavior.

jokes.text: You will never get work as an actor because you have no talent.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to psychoanalyze Lucille about denial
jokes.punchline: Her brutal, direct assessment of his acting career
jokes.explanation: Lucille's devastating honesty cuts through Tobias's pretensions. The casual cruelty delivered as fact is perfectly in character and brutally effective.

jokes.text: I know how to get rid of Ann. You put her in a room with Gangy. She'll criticize her away.
jokes.setup: Maeby offering to help George Michael with his Ann problem
jokes.punchline: Her solution is weaponizing Lucille's cruelty like a natural force
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight - treating Lucille's nastiness as a useful tool rather than a flaw.

jokes.text: What if I never get a chance to reach out and touch that hand of his again?
jokes.setup: George Sr. worried about Buster going to war
jokes.punchline: References their annual recreation of Michelangelo's Creation of Adam
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback that makes the art reference both touching and absurd.

jokes.text: Fine. Let me get Miss Fat Ass outta here and I'll meet you in the car.
jokes.setup: Maggie agreeing to talk to Michael about paternity
jokes.punchline: Her casual cruelty toward her client who she's supposedly defending
jokes.explanation: Shows Maggie's professional cynicism - she's defending someone she privately mocks. The casual cruelty reveals her true character.

jokes.text: You're a good uncle. I'm not your uncle. I'm your father. - What? - Shh. Shh. Quiet courage. Quiet courage. Hide.
jokes.setup: Buster praising Oscar for letting him hide from war
jokes.punchline: George Sr. drops a life-changing bombshell then immediately tells him to hide again
jokes.explanation: Huge emotional reveal immediately undermined by George Sr.'s terrible timing and advice.

jokes.text: But it didn't weigh 60 pounds.
jokes.setup: Lindsay finds the box that supposedly proves Maggie's pregnancy
jokes.punchline: The box marked '60 pounds' is easily lifted, revealing the deception
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective visual proof of deception. Economy of storytelling.

jokes.text: This one kinda does that too.
jokes.setup: Discussing a doll that cries when you pull its hair
jokes.punchline: Michael's casual comparison of Sally to a crying doll
jokes.explanation: Michael's offhand comparison of his girlfriend to a toy shows his detachment and casual cruelty. The understated delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Of course it is, Michael.
jokes.setup: Michael asking if the baby is his after she said it wasn't
jokes.punchline: Maggie suddenly reversing her position
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing for reveal that changes everything just as Michael was feeling confident

jokes.text: Okay, the box was light 'cause it had a fake stomach in it. It looks like she's faking it.
jokes.setup: Mystery of the light box revealed
jokes.punchline: Contains a pregnancy prosthetic, proving Maggie's deception
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the pregnancy mystery subplot. Functional plot advancement.

jokes.text: You know, when times are tough... you can always turn to the power of prayer. Here we go. - Show me.
jokes.setup: Ann offering religious comfort
jokes.punchline: George Michael's sarcastic 'here we go' followed by genuine interest
jokes.explanation: Shows George Michael's conflicted feelings about Ann's religiosity - mockery and curiosity.

jokes.text: Oh, you're so right, Michael. I really can't be trusted. 'Cause I'm fakin' it- not.
jokes.setup: Michael accusing Maggie of faking pregnancy
jokes.punchline: She admits to faking... then reveals she's not faking with dated slang
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and callback to Michael's dated language. Reveals the pregnancy is real.

jokes.text: Well, my wife and I... finally had the thrill of seeing a plus sign on a pregnancy test. You're gonna be a daddy!
jokes.setup: Tobias arriving with news about pregnancy test
jokes.punchline: He's talking about testing Maggie's urine, not Lindsay's, but phrases it like his own announcement
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - sounds like Tobias is announcing his own pregnancy before the reveal.

jokes.text: Loose seal! Watch out for loose seal! I don't care about Lucille! She lies! [Narrator] And then a seal bites off his hand.
jokes.setup: People warning about a loose seal while Buster rants about Lucille lying
jokes.punchline: He misunderstands 'loose seal' as 'Lucille' and gets attacked by an actual seal
jokes.explanation: Masterpiece of setup and payoff. The wordplay, visual pun, and story convergence are perfect. This is peak Arrested Development.
